channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Is Gibbs-White favourite to start as England's number 10 at World Cup?
item.description: Morgan Gibbs-White has enjoyed an impressive resurgence in 2026, so is the Nottingham Forest attacking midfielder now the leading contender for the number 10 role with England at the World Cup?

item.title: Everton fan arrested for alleged Semenyo racist abuse
item.description: An Everton fan is arrested by Merseyside Police during his side's match with Manchester City for allegedly racially abusing Antoine Semenyo.

item.title: What now for Rohl's Rangers as title hopes fade?
item.description: Rangers' Scottish Premiership title challenge has been undone by two damaging defeats by Motherwell and Hearts. What now for Danny Rohl and his players?

item.title: Foden reaches agreement over new Man City deal
item.description: England midfielder Phil Foden reaches an agreement in principle over a new four-year deal at Manchester City.

item.title: Derry says debut a 'dream come true' despite injury
item.description: The 18-year-old has been released from hospital after suffering a head injury during Chelsea's match against Nottingham Forest on Monday.

item.title: How did Guehi 'assist' allow Barry to score when offside?
item.description: Everton's Thierno Barry effectively gained from being offside when Manchester City's Marc Guehi teed him up to score on Monday. Is the law fair? And were the game's other big decisions correct?

item.title: O'Neill says Hearts can be regular title contenders
item.description: Celtic manager Martin O'Neill believes Hearts are in a strong position to become regular title contenders in the Scottish Premiership.

item.title: Adriano, Samba, R.Larcos - ranking the most iconic video game footballers
item.description: Fifa, Pro Evolution Soccer and Football Manager have been among the most popular football video games - but who are the most iconic players from them?

item.title: Napoli to make Hojlund's move permanent - Wednesday's gossip
item.description: Napoli will turn Man Utd striker Rasmus Hojlund's loan into a permanent move, West Ham winger Crysencio Summerville is being monitored by a number of clubs and Chelsea consider appointing Xavi Hernandez as their next manager.

item.title: Who has made Troy's Premier League team of the week?
item.description: After every round of Premier League matches this season, Troy Deeney gives us his team of the week. Do you agree with his choices?

item.title: Arteta pays tribute to Arsenal players & fans after 'incredible night'
item.description: Mikel Arteta says Arsenal "created history together" and praises the atmosphere and energy of the Emirates Stadium crowd, as Arsenal beat Atletico Madrid 2-1 on aggregate to reach their first Champions League final in 20 years.

item.title: Watch: Kane gives Dunfermline play-off advantage
item.description: Watch Chris Kane's goal in Dunfermline Athletic's 1-0 Scottish Premiership play-off quarter-final first-leg win against Arbroath.

item.title: Why Premier League title is Arsenal's to lose after Man City slip
item.description: Mark Chapman, Charlie Adam, Shay Given and Rory Smith discuss Manchester City’s 3-3 draw at Everton, focusing on how dropped points could impact their Premier League title challenge.

item.title: Man City's title hopes dented despite late Doku stunner in six-goal thriller
item.description: Jeremy Doku's 97th-minute equaliser salvages a draw for Manchester City in a six-goal thriller at Everton.

item.title: Nottingham Forest boost survival hopes with win over Chelsea
item.description: Nottingham Forest increase their hopes of Premier League survival after a 3-1 win over Chelsea at Stamford Bridge, as goals from Taiwo Awoniyi and Igor Jesus help the visitors open a six-point gap over 18th-placed West Ham in the relegation zone.

item.title: Doku scored 'really top goals' in 'really good game' - Guardiola
item.description: Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola says Jeremy Doku scored two "really, really top goals" in their six-goal thriller against Everton, as they lose ground on Arsenal in the Premier League title race.

item.title: 'Caption this' - Ferdinand and Carragher's social spat
item.description: Rio Ferdinand and Jamie Carragher may have been team-mates for England - but there is clearly no love lost between the former centre-backs based on a social media spat.

item.title: 'Pure theatre puts Hearts on cusp of title fairytale'
item.description: BBC Scotland chief sports writer Tom English sums up the chaos and cacophony of Hearts' win over Rangers at Tynecastle.

item.title: Forest hopeful of Gibbs-White fitness for Europa tie
item.description: Morgan Gibbs-White might need a "miracle" to be ready for Nottingham Forest's Europa League semi-final second leg - but his head coach Vitor Pereira is hopeful.

item.title: A remarkable finale - but will 13 chaotic minutes cost Man City title?
item.description: A chaotic 13-minute spell from Man City in the second half of a six-goal thriller at Everton could prove to be the visitors' undoing in the Premier League title race.

item.title: Man City reject Man Utd FA Youth Cup final venue offer
item.description: Manchester City will host their FA Youth Cup final with Manchester United at their 7,000-capacity Joie Stadium - rather than at Etihad Stadium.

item.title: Champions League quiz: How well do you remember last week's first legs?
item.description: There was a goalfest in Paris and VAR drama in Madrid, but how closely were you paying attention?

item.title: Rangers' Sterling banned from road after drink driving crash
item.description: Footballer Dujon Sterling has also been fined after crashing his car while almost three times over the drink driving limit.

item.title: What Champions League failure means for 'broken club' Chelsea
item.description: After a sixth-straight league loss, Chelsea are almost certain to miss out on Champions League qualification - a bitter blow which is likely to have far‑reaching consequences.

item.title: Seven new faces named in Republic of Ireland squad
item.description: Benfica attacker Jaden Umeh is one of seven new players named by Republic of Ireland head coach Heimir Hallgrimsson for this month's friendly against Grenada.

item.title: Moyes disappointed to only take point against Man City
item.description: EEverton boss David Moyes says he is "really disappointed" to come away with one point after Jeremy Doku scored a late equaliser for Manchester City in a 3-3 draw in the Premir League.

item.title: Late double from West Ham stuns Villa
item.description: West Ham clinch a 2-0 victory over Aston Villa in the Women's Super League after Riko Ueki scores in the 85th minute before substitute Seraina Piubel secures the win six minutes later.

item.title: We changed the players but kept the spirit - Pereira
item.description: Nottingham Forest manager Vitor Pereira says his side is "special" after beating Chelsea 3-1 and boosting their hopes of survival in the Premier League.

item.title: 'Massively disappointing' - McFarlane reflects on loss to Forest
item.description: Chelsea interim boss Calum McFarlane says Chelsea are "a lot better" than they showed in their 3-1 defeat by Nottingham Forest in the Premier League on Monday.

item.title: Man Utd beat Liverpool to secure Champions League place
item.description: Watch highlights as Manchester United secure their spot in the Champions League next season after a 3-2 win over Liverpool in the Premier League.

item.title: Spurs out of relegation zone with Villa win
item.description: Tottenham Hotspur propel themselves out of the relegation zone with a commanding 2-1 win over Aston Villa in the Premier League.

item.title: Has 'diamond' Shankland delivered his biggest moment for Hearts?
item.description: Lawrence Shankland is hailed as a "diamond of a player" after his superb winning goal against Rangers moved Hearts a step closer to a historic league title.

item.title: Will a fit Saka prove key to Arsenal's season finale?
item.description: With Bukayo Saka back from injury for Arsenal, could he prove the difference-maker in the finale to the Gunners' season?

item.title: Terzic agrees to become new coach of Athletic Club
item.description: Former Borussia Dortmund boss Edin Terzic agrees to become the new Athletic Club coach on a two-year contract.

item.title: Can stats pick out the Championship play-off winner?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks to see if statistics can help us to pick out the winner of the Championship play-offs.

item.title: Chester appoint manager with same name as boss of rivals Wrexham
item.description: Chester appoint former Altrincham boss Phil Parkinson on a two-year deal.

item.title: 'Cheers and tears of relief - it's never just a game'
item.description: BBC Radio Sheffield's Rob Staton reflects on the significance of a new era beginning at Hillsborough on Saturday.

item.title: The high-quality relegation battle more like a title race
item.description: The battle for Premier League survival is hotting up with Nottingham Forest, Tottenham and West Ham fighting for their lives.

item.title: Mourinho's Benfica still unbeaten - but miss out on title
item.description: Jose Mourinho's Benfica could go an entire season unbeaten - but it will not be enough to deliver the club a 39th Portuguese league title.

item.title: How De Zerbi brought life to sinking Spurs
item.description: BBC Sport tactics expert Umir Irfan looks at how Roberto de Zerbi has revived Premier League strugglers Tottenham.

item.title: 'Premflix' and tourist fans - future of football predictions
item.description: Former broadcasting executive Neil Duncanson and author Alex Fynn, who made remarkably accurate predictions about football's future in 1994, discuss what the game will look like in 10 years' time.

item.title: Hume reflects on 'whirlwind' rise after award win
item.description: Trai Hume says he has enjoyed a "whirlwind" rise in football after establishing himself for Sunderland and Northern Ireland.

item.title: Hurzeler agrees new long-term deal with Brighton
item.description: Fabian Hurzeler extends his stay as Brighton boss after agreeing a new long-term contract.

item.title: What happened to Everton's £40m signing Dibling?
item.description: The curious case of Tyler Dibling - BBC Sport chief football writer Phil McNulty asks what has happened to Everton's £40m youngster and what the holds.

item.title: The battle for sixth... and why teams might need to lose on final day
item.description: As the Premier League's battle for European places hots up, BBC Sport assesses the situation - and some very strange potential quirks in the race.

item.title: Carrick secures Champions League - what are Man Utd waiting for?
item.description: Unless Manchester United appoint Luis Enrique, it is hard to think of an acceptable alternative to giving Michael Carrick the job on a permanent basis, writes Simon Stone.

item.title: Are Ipswich better equipped for Premier League this time?
item.description: Ipswich Town have won promotion from the Championship for the second time in three years - but are they better equipped for the Premier League in 2026?

item.title: 'We got police escort to Vardy's party' - Leicester's 5,000-1 title win 10 years on
item.description: Premier League title-winning captain Wes Morgan and former Leicester City team-mate Marc Albrighton reflect on the club's 5,000-1 triumph a decade on.

item.title: 'I lost myself' - Bright on retirement, abuse and the future
item.description: Former England and Chelsea captain Millie Bright speaks to BBC Sport a day after announcing her retirement.

item.title: Ups, downs and the race for Europe
item.description: All you need to know about promotion, relegation and qualification for Europe in England and Scotland's top men's football leagues, and the Women's Super League.

item.title: All done deals since February, including deadline day
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: Who has qualified for next season's Champions League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains which teams have already qualified for the 2026-27 edition of the Champions League.

item.title: Which Premier League players are out of contract this summer?
item.description: With a World Cup summer fast approaching, BBC Sport looks at which Premier League stars may be looking for new clubs.

item.title: Why did Bayern Munich players wear cockatoo T-shirts?
item.description: Bayern Munich's players celebrated their 35th German top-flight title on Sunday with T-shirts displaying a cockatoo

item.title: How many points do you need to stay in the Premier League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks into the number of points needed to avoid relegation from the Premier League

item.title: How are the Scottish Premiership post-split fixtures decided?
item.description: The Scottish Premiership split has again been a controversial talking point, so why does it exist and why is it so complicated?

item.title: How does Scottish Premiership split work?
item.description: The Scottish Premiership title race has been a hot topic all season and it's about to become even more interesting with the league's split.

item.title: Have all home nations ever played in same World Cup?
item.description: With England and Scotland already qualified for the 2026 World Cup, Wales and Northern Ireland both face European qualifiers in March.

item.title: What is Scotland's most iconic goal? This time - McFadden v Tierney
item.description: Vote to find out what goal will be crowned Scotland's greatest.

item.title: Why Dundee United are signing A-League talent
item.description: Dundee United have signed Australian defender Joshua Rawlins for next season - their third recruit from the A-League already.

item.title: FC Basel postpone fixture after stadium fire
item.description: FC Basel's Swiss Super League match against FC Thun is postponed after a fire causes "significant damage" to Basel's St Jakob-Park stadium.

item.title: Spalletti signs new Juventus contract amid Italy links
item.description: Juventus manager Luciano Spalletti signs a two-year contract extension with the club, effectively ruling him out of a return to the Italy national team.

item.title: How Lens embraced underdog status to become PSG's title rivals
item.description: Lens have pushed PSG closer than anyone in Ligue 1 this season and, with the sides still to meet again, still hold an outside shot at the title.

item.title: Legendary manager Lucescu dies five days after resigning as Romania coach
item.description: Legendary manager Mircea Lucescu dies aged 80, five days after stepping down as Romania's head coach.

item.title: North Korean side to make rare trip to South Korea
item.description: A team of North Korean footballers will make a rare visit to South Korea later this month.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.

item.title: The great number nine decline - where have England's strikers gone?
item.description: BBC Sport takes an in-depth look at why England lack centre-forward options behind Harry Kane - a far cry from when the likes of Les Ferdinand only won 17 caps.

item.title: Captain, player, manager, Mister, number one - Davids at Barnet
item.description: BBC Sport remembers Edgar Davids' 15 months as Barnet player-manager in League Two and the Conference.

item.title: From farming village to Liverpool icon - the Salah story
item.description: BBC Sport visits Egypt to discover what Mohamed Salah means to the people of the football-mad country, and how a small boy from humble beginnings became a national icon.

item.title: Napoli 'smell blood' & target Premier League's discarded stars
item.description: After Scott McTominay was a hit, Napoli went back to the Premier League for Kevin de Bruyne, could more players follow them to Serie A?
